decks.notes: The deck focuses on controlling the game by using disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push, while also having aggressive creatures like Badgermole Cub and Sentinel of the Nameless City to pressure the opponent. The deck also includes powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Emeritus of Abundance to close out the game. The mana base is optimized for consistency and flexibility, with utility lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Wastewood Verge. Overall, the deck aims to grind out advantages through resource management and efficient removal, while maintaining a strong presence on the battlefield.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on speed and efficiency, using low-cost spells and creatures to quickly overwhelm the opponent. By playing aggressively with cards like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear, and backing them up with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent from the early turns of the game. Utilizing spells like Spell Pierce and Boomerang Basics to disrupt the opponent's strategy, the deck looks to push for victory before the opponent can stabilize.

The main concept of this Arclight Phoenix aggro deck is to quickly fill your graveyard with instant and sorcery spells, allowing you to bring back Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard multiple times in a single turn. This strategy is supported by cards like Consider, Opt, and Treasure Cruise to draw through your deck quickly, while also providing interaction with Lightning Axe and Fiery Impulse for removal. The deck aims to apply pressure early with efficient threats like Picklock Prankster and close out games by recurring Arclight Phoenix for massive burst damage.

The focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm opponents with a combination of efficient creatures and combat tricks. By utilizing cards like Sheltered by Ghosts to protect key creatures and Monstrous Rage to pump them up, the deck aims to deal quick and decisive damage to win the game before the opponent can set up their defenses. With a mix of aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Seasoned Hallowblade, along with burn spells like Reckless Rage, the deck is designed to put pressure on the opponent from the very beginning of the game.

This deck focuses on utilizing the combo potential of Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful board state. By using cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to disrupt the opponent's game plan, along with protection from Iron-Shield Elf, the deck aims to assemble the combo pieces for a game-winning play. Guardian of New Benalia provides additional support and Monument to Endurance helps to keep the deck's engine running smoothly. The mana base includes a mix of utility lands to support the deck's overall strategy.

The main concept of this Aggro Red Deck Wins Pioneer deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. By playing efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger alongside burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to deal damage quickly and efficiently. The deck also includes land cards like Ramunap Ruins and Mutavault to provide additional damage sources and reach. Overall, the strategy revolves around putting pressure on the opponent early and maintaining that pressure to secure a quick victory.

The key concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with low cost, high impact spells and aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage. The deck aims to control the board with removal spells like Reckless Rage and Fiery Impulse while dealing consistent damage with cheap spells like Wild Ride and Stormchaser's Talent. By maintaining a fast pace and applying pressure early on, the deck aims to outpace the opponent and secure a swift victory.

This UW Control deck focuses on countering spells, gaining card advantage, and stalling the game until it can establish board control with powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria. It also includes a variety of removal spells, card draw, and mana fixing to ensure it can handle a wide range of threats. By playing defensively and controlling the pace of the game, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and eventually secure victory through attrition. With a focus on control and resource management, this deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory through careful planning and precise execution.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with efficient creatures like Bishop of Wings, Llanowar Elves, and Skyclave Apparition. By utilizing cards like Collected Company and Kayla's Reconstruction, the deck can quickly refill the board after any board wipes. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and life gain effects, while also using Righteous Valkyrie and Resplendent Angel to close out games quickly. The mana base ensures consistent access to both white and green mana sources for casting spells on curve.

This deck focuses on quickly playing powerful legendary creatures to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. By utilizing a mix of efficient removal spells and powerful legendary creatures from five different colors, the deck aims to establish board presence early and maintain pressure throughout the game. With a combination of efficient removal and resilient threats, the deck seeks to maintain tempo and disrupt the opponent's game plan while steadily building towards a win condition through combat damage.

The deck focuses on assembling the Lotus Field combo to generate a large amount of mana and quickly ramp into powerful threats like Terror of the Peaks and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods. It also leverages cards like Scapeshift and Arboreal Grazer to accelerate its game plan, while utilizing cards like Aftermath Analyst and Formidable Speaker for card advantage and protection. The inclusion of cards like Underground Mortuary and Morlun, Devourer of Spiders help provide graveyard interaction and additional threats, making the deck versatile and challenging to interact with.

The deck revolves around utilizing low-cost Spirit creatures along with disruptive spells to maintain tempo and control the board. By deploying threats quickly and protecting them with counter spells and removal, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent while pressuring them with a constant stream of evasive creatures. The inclusion of Spirit tribal synergies and efficient creatures helps to maintain a proactive game plan, while also providing options for disruption and protecting key creatures. The deck focuses on maintaining a fast pace and keeping the opponent on the back foot, ultimately leading to a quick victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing the combo between Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful board state and overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards that support the combo such as Monument to Endurance and Fleeting Spirit, as well as removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse to control the field. The addition of Guardian of New Benalia and Iron-Shield Elf provide additional protection and utility. The deck aims to quickly establish a dominant position and win the game through a combination of aggressive plays and strategic resource management.

The main focus of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan through a combination of hand disruption (Thoughtseize, Duress), removal spells (Fatal Push, Abrupt Decay), and creature control (Sentinel of the Nameless City, Graveyard Trespasser). The deck aims to slowly grind out advantages with efficient removal and resilient threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Culling Ritual and Tear Asunder provides additional flexibility in dealing with a variety of threats. Overall, the deck prioritizes resource management and board control to outvalue the opponent over the course of the game.

The focus of this deck is to quickly cast spells, trigger Arclight Phoenix's ability to return from the graveyard, and maintain pressure on the opponent with aggressive plays. The deck includes card draw and selection spells to help find Arclight Phoenix and other key cards, along with removal spells to deal with threats. The goal is to outpace the opponent by casting multiple spells in a turn, keeping up the pressure and overwhelming them with efficient creatures and disruptive spells.

The main concept of this Arclight Phoenix Aggro deck is to quickly cast spells and efficiently cycle through the deck to bring back Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard. By utilizing cards like Artist's Talent, Consider, Opt, and Fiery Impulse, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast and consistent damage while maintaining card advantage through spells like Treasure Cruise. The inclusion of Flashback and Into the Flood Maw provides additional options for spell recursion and disruption, allowing the deck to control the game tempo and outpace the opponent.
